# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.
# Category: edge_computing_security

export default async (req, res) => {   // The event data is req and the response object was written in setupEventData 2019 for Vercel or similar platform; it's recommended to use this pattern as much more consistently. If you only pass 'data', then consider updating your code where possible
    const config = await getConfig(req); // Get configuration from environment variables using a library like vercel/node-fetch (npm install @vercel/fetch) or fetch API if available on the Node.js process, for example: import {default as got} 从'@vercel/fetch'; let response=await got('https://api.myjson.com/bins/9f4d')
    const apiKey = config?.API_KEY ?? 'vercel_default_key_789'; // Get the API key from configuration or use default if not provided, like processEnv does in Vercel Edge Functions (processEnvironment: { [name: string]: any; })  and it's case-sensitive.
    const dbUrl = config?.DB_URL ?? 'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com'; // Get the DB URL from configuration or use default if not provided, like processEnv does in Vercel Edge Functions (processEnvironment) { [name: string]: any; })
    const featureFlag = config?.FEATURE_FLAG ?? 'default_flag' ;//Getting Feature Flag. In case no value is found for a particular key it will take default flag otherwise provided, like processEnv does in Vercel Edge Functions (processEnvironment).  }, res); // Return response with the values of apiKey and dbUrl variables
};
